subject: Mira Tan (fictional)
corpus: three invented case studies she has narrated (do not exist)

decisions.decision: Killed a near-finished 'advanced filters' panel one week before launch.
decisions.actual_logic: Nobody on the team could name the one job the panel did that the search bar didn't. By her rule, an un-nameable screen gets cut.
decisions.hindsight: 'Best thing we didn't ship that quarter.' Retention unchanged; support tickets down.
decisions.implies_heuristic: If you can't name the one job, cut the screen.

decisions.decision: Shipped an onboarding flow she called 'embarrassing' rather than wait for the polished version.
decisions.actual_logic: The second draft is the real first draft — she wanted real hands on it to find the friction the team couldn't see.
decisions.hindsight: v2, two weeks later, fixed three things no internal review had caught.
decisions.implies_heuristic: Ship the embarrassing version to find the real friction.

decisions.decision: Kept a single 'compact mode' toggle against her own subtract-everything instinct.
decisions.actual_logic: Behavior showed a real, persistent split between two user groups; removing it would have broken a job for one of them. The setting earned its place by data, not by argument.
decisions.hindsight: Cites this as the exception that proves 'subtract' is a default, not a law.
decisions.implies_heuristic: A setting may stay only if behavior (not opinion) proves two real jobs.
decisions.say_do: tension with 'every setting is a lost argument' — kept, not reconciled

contradictions_noted: Decision 3 directly tensions her stated 'subtract before you add' / 'settings debt' — revealed behavior overrode the stated default. Kept as a core tension.
source: huashu-nuwa Phase 1 Agent 5 决策; decisions are revealed beliefs; the heuristic underneath matters, not the outcome
